caption:
Google removes reviews that violate its solicitation policy, and it does it automatically, with no warning.

Two practices trigger it fast. On-site kiosks or tablets that screen customers by sentiment before sending the review link. Scripts that tell customers what to say, like naming a specific employee.

The fix takes three changes. Pull the tablet from your front desk. Cut the "mention Dave" line from your review script. Send the review link by text a few hours after the job, not while the customer is standing in front of you.

Same open-ended ask, sent to every customer, sent later. That keeps your reviews on the map and your profile out of Google's enforcement queue.

Want a review-ask system built to stay compliant and still bring in reviews every week?
DM "REVIEWS".

#GoogleBusinessProfile #ReviewGeneration #LocalServiceMarketing #ReputationManagement #ServiceBusinessGrowth
rationale: Reframed from an unverifiable 'April 2026 update' and a fabricated removal statistic to Google's actual, longstanding review-gating and solicitation policy. Keeps the carousel format for the compliance checklist and still delivers a concrete 3-step fix owners can act on today.
